claims_verified.claim: The un-stick rule is table-driven; _recorded_terminal consults clears_terminal and hardcodes no event name
RESUMES + clears_terminal live in terminals.py; MERGE_DECIDED matched by the shared prefix constant
A newer director_resume un-sticks CAP_REACHED/ESCALATED_DEADLOCK/PUSHED/MERGE_DECIDED and push:ok; order is load-bearing
claims_verified.evidence: live: _recorded_terminal([...,terminal CAP_REACHED,resume]) is None; [resume,...,terminal CAP_REACHED]=='CAP_REACHED'; suite 719 passed

claims_verified.claim: PATH_GUARD_VIOLATION and unknown states stay sticky
director_resume not in _PHASE_ACTIONS; derive next_phase/round unchanged
claims_verified.evidence: orchestrator/loop.py:78; live: derive with/without resume both yield phase='developer', round=2, sessions preserved

claims_verified.claim: The note is prepended additively in _run_developer and self-clears after a phase action
CLI validation is all-or-nothing; nothing written on empty/missing reason, PATH_GUARD_VIOLATION, no-terminal, unknown task
Resume path never pushes to origin/merges/skips a reviewer; spec_sha written but read nowhere in decision logic
claims_verified.evidence: grep spec_sha -> only run.py:421-423 (write); gate_states/_decide_merge ignore director_resume (loop.py:905-922); test_resume_path_does_not_push_or_merge_or_skip_review passes

claims_verified.claim: Receipt shows count + latest reason and excludes director_resume from the round trace
Suite green, ruff clean, basedpyright 0 errors for touched scope
claims_verified.evidence: pytest -q -> 719 passed; ruff check orchestrator/ tests/ -> All checks passed; basedpyright -> 0 errors, 229 pre-existing reportAny warnings

claims_verified.claim: Spec-cited live example mcp (MERGE_DECIDED:stop_before_merge) un-sticks but the resumed loop derives 'done', not a developer round
claims_verified.evidence: terminal_spec('MERGE_DECIDED:stop_before_merge').kind=='success' so _finalize appends push:ok (loop.py:862-865); live derive_resume_point([...,push:ok,terminal MERGE_DECIDED,resume]).phase=='done'; loop.py:808-823 re-records+returns without _run_developer

findings.summary: Resuming a PUSHED or MERGE_DECIDED:* terminal un-sticks it but the loop derives phase 'done' (from the trailing push:ok that _finalize appends for success-kind terminals), so it re-records the same terminal and returns with NO developer round — the corrected spec is never acted on and DIRECTOR_NOTE_SECTION is silently dropped. Round-trip steps 5-7 and the Goal ('hand the developer a corrected spec and a written note') are unmet for these states, including the spec's own cited live example.
findings.failure_scenario: Director runs `kickoff mcp --resume --reason "spec was missing throttling; added it"` on the cited mcp branch (last terminal MERGE_DECIDED:stop_before_merge, preceded by push:ok). _recorded_terminal returns None (un-stuck), but derive_resume_point returns phase='done' (transitions[('push','ok')]=='done'); _run_phases hits the 'done' branch (loop.py:808-823), re-appends terminal MERGE_DECIDED, re-pushes the task branch, and returns — _run_developer never runs, so the amended spec is ignored and the note reason is never delivered. Identical for a plain PUSHED terminal.

findings.summary: Resuming an ESCALATED_DEADLOCK terminal un-sticks it, but _override_phase re-detects the still-present nonconvergence fingerprints and (senior_ran true) routes phase 'developer' -> 'deadlock', re-terminating as ESCALATED_DEADLOCK before any developer round runs — the corrected spec and note are never applied.
findings.failure_scenario: A task ended ESCALATED_DEADLOCK after repeated rw2 nogo (same fingerprint) following a senior verdict. Director runs `--phase resume --reason "..."`. _recorded_terminal returns None; derive_resume_point returns 'developer'; but _override_phase (loop.py:701-703) sees nonconvergence_detected(entries) true (repeated fingerprints since the last senior remain in the log) and senior_ran true, so it returns 'deadlock' -> _finalize(ESCALATED_DEADLOCK) (loop.py:806-807). No developer round runs and DIRECTOR_NOTE_SECTION is never rendered; the resume is a no-op re-terminate.

findings.summary: When the last terminal is a retryable/non-table state (QUOTA_TIMEOUT, INTERNAL_ERROR), `--phase resume` refuses with a message asserting 'the tree is poisoned - discard the branch and restart', which is inaccurate/misleading for a transient terminal that should simply be re-kickoffed. Consider distinguishing 'poisoned (PATH_GUARD_VIOLATION)' from 'already retryable — re-kickoff normally'.

AC4's grep clause ('no event name is hardcoded in loop.py') is only enforced against _recorded_terminal's source; _director_note (loop.py:153) legitimately hardcodes 'director_resume'. The un-stick rule itself is correctly table-driven (AC4 intent met), but the note-rendering exception to the literal wording deserves an explicit comment/test note so it isn't mistaken for the bespoke-if anti-pattern the spec warns against.

test_assessment: AC1-3,5 covered by tests/test_terminals.py + test_loop_director_resume.py (parametrized un-stick, order/one-run, PATH_GUARD_VIOLATION, prefix with novel suffix). AC4 covered by a table-driven test over RESUMES plus a source-grep against _recorded_terminal only. AC6 covered (derive unchanged with/without resume, sessions preserved). AC7 covered by pure _director_note tests + one integration round asserting note AND rw1 verdict both reach the prompt and the note self-clears. AC8/AC9 covered by five CLI refusal tests (each asserting nothing written) + a happy-path stub asserting exactly one event with reason+spec_sha and clarify-skipped loop entry. AC10 covered (summary/handback receipt + trace exclusion). AC11 covered by a source-inspection guard. AC12 covered. AC13 verified: 719 passed, ruff clean, basedpyright 0 errors. Every new pure function has failure-mode tests EXCEPT the end-to-end resume behaviour of the non-CAP_REACHED terminals — no test drives a resumed PUSHED/MERGE_DECIDED/ESCALATED_DEADLOCK task through _run_phases to confirm a developer round actually runs, which is exactly where the two blockers hide (AC1 checks only that _recorded_terminal returns None, not that the loop then does productive work).
residual_risks: Of the four terminals director_resume claims to resume, only CAP_REACHED productively re-runs the developer; PUSHED, MERGE_DECIDED:*, and ESCALATED_DEADLOCK un-stick but immediately re-terminate without a developer round (the two blockers). Because the spec puts transition-table and nonconvergence-slicing changes out of scope, resolving this likely needs a spec-level decision (narrow director_resume's frozenset, or define how 'done'/'deadlock'-deriving resumes reach a developer round).

task_worktree returns an existing local worktree without fetching, so a spec the Director edited-and-pushed elsewhere may not be present at resume time and spec_sha then records the stale blob. Pre-existing worktree behaviour, not introduced here, but it bears on whether 'the ask changed' is truly reflected.

Trust surface holds: director_resume/spec_sha are inert in gate_states_from_log and _decide_merge; the resume CLI neither pushes to origin nor merges nor skips a reviewer; a forged resume from a compromised VPS only buys more of its own compute.
